latest.title: Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
latest.content:
18 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7386I
ProCook Group PLC
18 June 2026
18 June 2026
ProCook Group plc
Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
ProCook Group plc ("ProCook" or "the Group"), the UK's leading direct-to-consumer specialist kitchenware brand,
is pleased to announce that Lee Tappenden, CEO and Dan Walden, CFO will provide a live presentation relating to its FY26 Annual Results via Investor Meet Company on 24 Jun 2026 at 12:00 BST.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 23 Jun 2026, 09:00 BST, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet PROCOOK GROUP PLC via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/procook-group-plc/register-investor
Investors who already follow PROCOOK GROUP PLC on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
For further information please contact:
ProCook Group plc:
investor.relations@procook.co.uk
Lee Tappenden, Chief Executive Officer
Dan Walden, Chief Financial Officer
MHP Group (Financial PR Adviser):
procook@mhpgroup.com
Tel: +44 (0)7884 494 112
Katie
Hunt
Lucy
Gibbs
Finn
Taylor
Notes to editors:
ProCook is the UK's leading direct-to-consumer specialist kitchenware brand. ProCook designs, develops, and retails a high-quality range of direct-sourced and own-brand kitchenware which provides customers with significant value for money.
The brand sells directly through its website,
www.procook.co.uk
, and through 79 own-brand retail stores, located across the UK.
Founded 30 years ago as a family business, selling cookware sets by direct mail in the UK, ProCook has grown into a market leading, multi-channel specialist kitchenware company, employing over 700 colleagues, and operating from its Store Support Centre in Gloucester.
As a B Corp, a Real Living Wage employer and a certified Great Place to Work™, ProCook is committed to being a socially responsible and environmentally conscious business for the benefit of all stakeholders.
ProCook has been listed on the London Stock Exchange since November 2021 (PROC.L).
Further information about the ProCook Group can be found at
www.procookgroup.co.uk
.

latest.title: Notice of Full Year Results Date
latest.content:

RNS Number : 9236H
ProCook Group PLC
11 June 2026
11 June 2026
ProCook Group plc
Notice of Full Year Results Date
ProCook Group plc ("ProCook" or "the Group"), the UK's leading direct-to-consumer specialist kitchenware brand,
will issue its Annual Results for the 52 weeks ended 29 March 2026 on Wednesday, 24 June 2026 at 07:00 BST.
ProCook's management will host an in-person presentation for analysts and institutional investors at 09:00 BST at Singer Capital Markets' offices, 1 Bartholomew Ln, London, EC2N 2AX, on the day. For those who are unable to attend the presentation in person, a live webcast will also be available.
A recording of the Annual Results webcast will also be made available on the ProCook website.

latest.title: Fourth quarter trading update
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2723A
ProCook Group PLC
14 April 2026
14 April 2026
ProCook Group plc
Fourth quarter trading update
Trading performance ahead of expectations, significantly outperforming the market, driving improved profitability year on year
Strong cash generation supporting investment for growth as we continue to deliver on our strategy
ProCook Group plc ("ProCook" or "the Group"), the UK's leading direct-to-consumer specialist kitchenware brand, today reports on Q4 trading results for the 12 weeks ended 29 March 2026 and the full year (unaudited1).
Quarter four
Year to date
£m
FY26
YoY2
FY26
YoY2
Revenue
18.5
19.2%

5.7%
Excellent trading performance resulting in substantial market share gains
·
Total revenue in Q4 increased by +19.2% to £18.5m, with total like for like revenue increasing by +9.9%
o
Retail revenue increased by +19.4%, reflecting an eleventh consecutive quarter of like for like growth (+3.9%) benefitting from our focus on service excellence, with new stores contributing a further +15.5% points of revenue growth
o
Ecommerce revenue increased by +18.9%,
with like for like growth of +18.2%
primarily driven by increased traffic. Marketplace revenue contributed +0.7% points of growth
·
Record full year revenue of £85.5m, ahead of market expectations4, +23.0% year on year and +11.8% on a like for like basis
·
Outperformed the UK kitchenware market by +13% points during Q4, and by +20% across the full year5
·
EBITDA anticipated to be slightly ahead of market expectations for the full year driven by trading performance and acceleration in new store openings
·
Operating profit anticipated to be in line with market expectations reflecting the dilutive impact of new stores prior to reaching maturity, and pre-opening costs. PBT also anticipated to be in line with expectations, after impact of significant FX volatility
·
Strengthened balance sheet with net cash position of £4.4m at year end (FY25: net cash of £1.0m) ahead of market expectations, after £5.3m of investment capex on new store openings and disciplined management of working capital
·
£20.4m of available liquidity in cash and facilities. RCF increased to £15m (previously £10m) shortly after year end and committed for a further three years to April 2029
Continued investment to build a stronger business and deliver our medium term ambitions
·
Three new stores opened in the fourth quarter taking the full year total to 13 (net 12, after one planned closure in Q1), increasing our total UK retail estate to 78 stores, of which eight are now in our new format
·
Investment in paid media to accelerate growth in customer base supporting a significant increase in new customer acquisition (+25% YoY) and L12M active customer base (+24%)
·
Acceleration in social media content creation supporting growth in paid social revenue (over +67% YoY in Q4), enhanced by acquisition of substantial digital content library (9000+ recipes and imagery) from UK operators (in liquidation) of the Delicious magazine
·
Continue to improve our technology, with a programme launched in February and expected to be phased over the next two years to support growth, increase agility and accelerate the capture of AI benefits
Lee Tappenden, Chief Executive Officer, commented:
"We have delivered a strong fourth quarter and full year performance, significantly outperforming the market and improving profitability, whilst accelerating investment in our new store opening programme. Strong revenue growth across both our expanding store footprint and online reflects substantial increases in new customers attracted to our brand and repeat purchases, demonstrating that our unique product proposition and service focus is really resonating with consumers.
"Whilst we are mindful of the potential macroeconomic effects of any protracted geopolitical instability, we look forward to building on our recent progress and continuing to increase market share as we drive profitable growth. Our ongoing store openings, initiatives to increase brand awareness, and disciplined investments to support growth, position us well to deliver on our medium term ambition of 100 stores, £100m revenue and 10% operating profit margin."
For further information please contact:
ProCook Group plc
Lee Tappenden, Chief Executive Officer
Dan Walden, Chief Financial Officer
investor.relations@procook.co.uk
MHP Group (Financial PR Adviser)
Katie Hunt

procook@mhpgroup.com
Tel: +44 (0)7884 494 112
Next scheduled event:
ProCook expects to release its FY26 annual results on 24 June 2026.

The brand sells directly through its website,
www.procook.co.uk
, and through 78 own-brand retail stores, located across the UK.
Founded 30 years ago as a family business, selling cookware sets by direct mail in the UK, ProCook has grown into a market leading, multi-channel specialist kitchenware company, employing over 700 colleagues, and operating from its Store Support Centre in Gloucester.
As a B Corp, a Real Living Wage employer and a certified Great Place to WorkTM, ProCook is committed to being a socially responsible and environmentally conscious business for the benefit of all stakeholders.
ProCook has been listed on the London Stock Exchange since November 2021 (PROC.L).
Further information about the ProCook Group can be found at

latest.title: PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
Today 12:49
RNS Number : 0072Q
ProCook Group PLC
22 January 2026
22 January 2026
ProCook Group plc
PDMR Shareholding
ProCook Group plc (the "Company"), the UK's leading direct-to-consumer specialist kitchenware brand, announces that Daniel O'Neill, Non-Executive Director and Deputy Chair, transferred 1,510,000 ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") from a brokerage account into his own name.
Daniel O'Neill's beneficial interest in the Ordinary Shares did not change as a result of the transfer detailed above and, together with Daniel O'Neill's connected persons, their total beneficial interest in the Company remains at 38,846,902, representing 35.65% of the Company's issued share capital.
The information in this notification is disclosed in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation.
For further information please contact:
ProCook Group plc
Lee Tappenden, Chief Executive Officer
Dan Walden, Chief Financial Officer
investor.relations@procook
.co.uk
MHP Group (Financial PR Adviser)
Katie Hunt
Lucy Gibbs
procook@mhpgroup.com
Tel: +44 (0)7885 447 944

The brand sells directly through its website, www.procook.co.uk, and through 75 own-brand retail stores, located across the UK.
Founded over 25 years ago as a family business, selling cookware sets by direct mail in the UK, ProCook has grown into a market leading, multi-channel specialist kitchenware company, employing over 700 colleagues, and operating from its Store Support Centre in Gloucester.
As a B Corp, a Real Living Wage employer and a certified Great Place to WorkTM, ProCook is committed to being a socially responsible and environmentally conscious business for the benefit of all stakeholders.
ProCook has been listed on the London Stock Exchange since November 2021 (PROC.L).
Further information about the ProCook Group can be found at www.procookgroup.co.uk.

company_30s.what_they_do: PROC.L runs a chain of small‑scale, family‑owned kitchenware and cookware outlets. The brand focuses on affordable, high‑quality pots, pans and cooking accessories that are sold both in physical stores and online.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The home‑cooking market is growing as consumers spend more time preparing meals at home. PROC.L’s niche positioning—family‑owned, local stores with a loyal regional customer base—gives it a defensible moat against generic e‑commerce players. Its product line taps into a broad, everyday‑use segment that is resilient during economic shifts.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a rebuilding phase. It has been expanding its retail footprint (five new stores slated for Christmas) while managing cash burn from recent openings. Share price has collapsed, but the business is still operating and has a clear path to profitability once the new stores mature.

rns_narrative: PROC.L is gearing up for its full‑year earnings release, which will show whether the new store rollout is improving revenue and margins. The appointment of a sole corporate broker signals a push for better liquidity and a potential share buy‑back or new equity offering.

The company has announced a series of incremental store openings, indicating confidence in its growth model. It also highlighted that the latest quarter’s results were ‘aggressive but necessary’ to support the expansion, suggesting a short‑term hit to profit that should reverse once the stores generate steady cash flow.
The narrative now revolves around two themes: (1) a tactical expansion strategy that could lift revenue, and (2) a corporate restructuring aimed at improving capital efficiency.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism from a niche community of loyal shoppers and small‑cap investors who see a hidden store‑rollout catalyst.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices praise the family‑owned culture and the new store openings as a clear sign of growth. They argue the price is a bargain after the recent crash.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter focuses on the company’s cash burn and the risk that the new stores may not generate the expected traffic. Some fear that the expansion could dilute margins further before the business becomes profitable.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed muted; no dramatic spikes in discussion. The lack of noise suggests the market has not yet priced in the upcoming earnings or store openings, leaving room for a surprise move.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for PROC.L is flat—no recent spikes. A dead trend means the market is still unaware of the company’s next move, maximizing the potential for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: Full‑year results on 11 June 2026: If revenue growth and margin improvement beat expectations, the market will see a clear turnaround signal.
Announcement of the first new store opening (expected early July): A successful launch would validate the expansion strategy and lift sentiment.
Corporate broker appointment could lead to a small equity offering or a buy‑back, tightening supply and supporting price.
Any positive commentary from key suppliers or a partnership that expands the product line could add credibility to the growth narrative.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals over the next 2–3 months include: (1) earnings beat on revenue and EBITDA, (2) a surge in trading volume around the results date, (3) a price break above the 20‑day moving average, and (4) any mention of a formal share buy‑back or new capital raise.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score of 9.0 indicates a tight price compression, suggesting the market is still in a holding phase after the crash. The 86% drawdown shows we’re likely at or near the bottom of a deep capitulation cycle. Confirmation will come when the stock starts moving away from the low and shows sustained volume.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen over 85% from its peak, a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically offers the biggest rebounds. If the price recovers to the midpoint of the drop (roughly 43% below the peak), it would still be 43% below the all‑time high—an upside of about 86% from the current level. Historically, RED and PURPLE signals deliver 150–200% gains once the catalyst arrives, so a 100% rebound is realistic.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s expansion is still in early stages, and cash burn remains high. If the new stores fail to attract enough customers, margins could deteriorate further, pushing the price back down. The worst‑case scenario is that the stock stays below its recent low for another year, limiting upside to less than 20%.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current price (the stock is already deep). Potential gain could be 100% if the price doubles, giving a risk‑reward ratio of at least 1:1, but the asymmetric nature of the setup means the upside potential far outweighs the downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed break above the 20‑day moving average and a volume spike of at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Allocate 5–10% of your discretionary capital. Place a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout. Use a trailing stop that locks in gains once the price moves up 20% from entry.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger a small position if the earnings release beats revenue by at least 5% or if the company announces a new store opening. Watch for a volume surge of 2× the 20‑day average around these events.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable. No delisting risk or bankruptcy filings are evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: PROC.L sits at a deep 86% crash; the next earnings release or first new store launch could spark a rebound, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play.
